title: Roll for It
editorialSummary: Dice games grouped by core mechanic, where every game plausibly fits two groups at first glance. Betting/banking (craps, sic bo, chuck-a-luck, hazard) — every game involves a banker and players bet against the house. Push-your-luck (farkle, yahtzee, zombie dice, pass the pigs) — roll and decide whether to bank or re-roll. Matching/collection (bunco, boggle dice, perudo, Mexico) — players aim to collect matching results or beat a target pattern. Elimination (ship captain crew, left-center-right, pig, shut the box) — players lose tokens/turns until eliminated. Hard because Yahtzee looks like a matching game, Craps looks like elimination, and Perudo looks like betting.
